name: Basic Life Support · CPR
summary: Unresponsive person, not breathing normally. Chest compressions keep oxygenated blood circulating.
recognition: Tap gently and ask if they can hear you — no response
Not breathing normally (occasional gasps don't count)
Often after collapse, accident, or cardiac event

callEmergency.text: Call 999 immediately. Activate speaker. Send someone for AED if available. If alone — start CPR first, get to a phone after 1 min for children/infants.

steps: Check it's safe to approach — don't become a casualty yourself.
Tap the person and shout to check response.
Open the airway: hand on forehead, two fingers on the bony jaw, tilt head back and lift chin.
Look, listen, feel for normal breathing for no more than 10 seconds.
If breathing normally → recovery position, call 999, monitor.
If not breathing normally → call 999 (speaker on), get AED if available, start chest compressions.
Kneel beside them. Heel of one hand in centre of chest (lower half of breastbone). Other hand on top, fingers interlocked.
Arms straight. Press down 5–6 cm (2–2.5 in). Release fully without lifting hands.
30 compressions at 100–120 per minute.
Optional: 2 rescue breaths after every 30 compressions — head tilt, pinch nose, seal mouth, breathe steadily for 1 second, watch chest rise.
Continue 30:2 until help arrives or the person starts breathing normally.
If unwilling to give rescue breaths — compressions only is fine.
Open airway — tilt head back gently, don't press on soft tissue under chin.
Look, listen, feel for breathing.
If no breathing — give 5 INITIAL RESCUE BREATHS first (pinch nose, seal mouth, 1-second breath, chest rises).
Then 30 chest compressions: heel of one hand in lower third of breastbone, push down about 5 cm (one-third chest depth). Use both hands if needed for depth.
Then 2 rescue breaths.
Continue 30:2.
If alone — do 1 minute of CPR before leaving to call 999.
Place infant on firm surface (waist-high table or floor).
Open airway to NEUTRAL position (don't tilt head back too far — this can compromise their airway).
Look, listen, feel for breathing.
If no breathing — give 5 INITIAL RESCUE BREATHS: seal your mouth over BOTH the baby's nose and mouth, breathe for 1 second.
Then 30 chest compressions: two fingers in lower third of breastbone, push down about 4 cm (one-third chest depth).
Then 2 rescue breaths.
Continue 30:2.
If alone — do 1 minute of CPR. You can take the infant with you to the phone while continuing CPR if needed.

warnings.text: Don't tilt an infant's head too far back — it can block their airway.
Compressions only are fine if you can't or won't give rescue breaths. The chest compressions are the most important part.
